fields.summary: Expose LOGDIR that containers should use for logging

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

issuetype.description: A problem which impairs or prevents the functions of the product.
issuetype.name: Bug

fields.description: The LOGDIR is known. We should expose this to the container's environment.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

comments.body: Duplicate of YARN-772.

I read this as more than just documentation. It is about exposing the final LOGDIR as env to the containers so that they can decide where to write logs at run-time.

As of today, AMs do have a not-so-straight way of doing this: an AM can explicitly set SOMEENV=<LOGDIR> and we promptly do the appropriate string replacement. But I think it is useful to set it explicitly.
Patch, set the final LOG_DIR into container's env and add a test.

{color:green}+1 overall{color}.  Here are the results of testing the latest attachment 
  http://issues.apache.org/jira/secure/attachment/12587757/YARN-781.patch
  against trunk revision .

    {color:green}+1 @author{color}.  The patch does not contain any @author tags.

    {color:green}+1 tests included{color}.  The patch appears to include 1 new or modified test files.

    {color:green}+1 javac{color}.  The applied patch does not increase the total number of javac compiler warnings.

    {color:green}+1 javadoc{color}.  The javadoc tool did not generate any warning messages.

    {color:green}+1 eclipse:eclipse{color}.  The patch built with eclipse:eclipse.

    {color:green}+1 findbugs{color}.  The patch does not introduce any new Findbugs (version 1.3.9) warnings.

    {color:green}+1 release audit{color}.  The applied patch does not increase the total number of release audit warnings.

    {color:green}+1 core tests{color}.  The patch passed unit tests in hadoop-yarn-project/hadoop-yarn/hadoop-yarn-api hadoop-yarn-project/hadoop-yarn/hadoop-yarn-server/hadoop-yarn-server-nodemanager.

    {color:green}+1 contrib tests{color}.  The patch passed contrib unit tests.

Test results: https://builds.apache.org/job/PreCommit-YARN-Build/1236//testReport/
Console output: https://builds.apache.org/job/PreCommit-YARN-Build/1236//console

This message is automatically generated.

On top of Sandy's comments, I have a few:
 - LOG_DIR should instead be LOG_DIRS? It is definitely a list, but the container itself is made to write to one disk if the <LOGDIR> tag is used. But if container writes some logs beyond that, it is worth distributing them across disks. I quickly checked and log-aggregation, log-serving etc. take care of multiple dirs.
 - If you agree to the above, the test should also validate multiple comma-separated list of dirs.

Thanks for Sandy, Vinod review.
New patch, add the the comment and expose the whole logDirs to the env
